title: Level 10.99
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 10.99: "Down In Flames"}}
{{Image Banner|image=1099-1.png}}
{{Class|p1=3|p2=5|p3=3}}
Description
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Blurimage|file=1099-1.png|caption=The fire.}}
'''Level 10.99,''' often referred to as "Down In Flames" or "Burning Field", is a flaming field that is endlessly long and continuously growing. The field is completely uninhabitable due to the fire, and while there are a few outposts, they are all used for study. [[B.W.O.]] has spent years trying to extinguish the fires of Level 10.99 and make it habitable, but to no avail, as the flames are spreading too fast and it is speculated that the speed of fire spread has increased exponentially, especially in the last two decades. Although the level appears to consist of an empty field, buildings or structures can occasionally be found. The most common structures are burning houses, towers that seem to stretch on forever, and fences that are found at random places. None of these structures contain anything useful to wanderers, and staying inside them would be quite difficult due to the extreme heat present throughout the level. Therefore, interacting with these structures would be a waste of time for wanderers, given the limited time they have to spend in the level. {{Blurimage|file=1099-3.png|caption=A relatively less burnt area, a B.W.O. explorer is seen in the photo.}} The level has no water at all, yet studies have revealed that it was originally covered in lakes. This has led to theories that the level was either unnaturally burned. It is quite uncommon to run into another wanderer on the level as there are not many people there and those that are usually lose their lives due to weather, fire or smoke. Entering the level is strongly discouraged and in case of unintentional entry it is recommended to seek an exit immediately. Wandering around aimlessly is completely unnecessary and once one enters the level, they should focus on their objective and act quickly. It is recommended to carry plenty of water for the hot weather. The sun never sets on the level, and it is usually in the position it is in [[The Frontrooms]] around 11:00 AM in the summer time. Due to the level of smoke in the level, the rate of disease among the wanderers is very high and this rate is increasing. Many of the wanderers who left the level were sick, some of them received medical treatment while others could not, and a significant number of them lost their lives. Attempts to measure the temperature of the level have failed because it is hotter beyond what can be measured. After a while, this temperature level exceeds what can be tolerated and can cause both physical and psychological damage, which is often temporary. The level, obviously, does not have any resources and the wanderers are expected to enter the level prepared. Canned food is not recommended as it may explode due to the high heat. It has been noted that involuntary entry into the level has become increasingly common in recent months. However, despite the increase in the number of wanderers present in the level, it has been observed that two individuals cannot meet unless they enter the level together. This situation not only makes establishing a support base within the level entirely impossible but also threatens the mental health of the wanderers. Although it is not known who discovered the level individually, the records state that it was first discovered by [[B.W.O.]] under the leadership of Henry Louis. Afterwards, [[B.W.O.]] agreed with [[M.E.G.]] to study this level and every month a group of [[B.W.O.]] and [[M.E.G.]] researchers are sent to the level to study the level. This practice is still ongoing today and the level is still being studied.

The Entrance
[[File:1099-2.png|300px|link=]]

As much as I want to forget what I've been through, my boss is forcing me to remember and document everything. I am a B.W.O. agent. You don't need to know my name or any information about me. All you need to know is that I am a researcher who was sent to Level 10.99 and had the worst experience of my life within three hours. My goal was to measure the level's size, and to do that, the three of us researchers had to advance as far as we could within the level. When I entered the level, memories from years ago came flooding back. It was just like Level 10, which I hadn't visited in years. The only difference was that now everything was on fire. My eyes were dazzled. I was sweating; it was too hot to be comfortable. I almost caught fire while walking. It was hard to breathe. The same could be said for my two friends beside me. We walked for a long time, exhausted. All three of us were about to pass out. We were convinced this level would go on forever. I told my friends we should turn back, but they insisted we could keep going. After walking a little further, I noticed that the flames and heat around us were increasing. I couldn't see through the flames anymore. There was no way we could go any further on this path. The flames surrounded all three of us. One of my friends was caught in the fire while trying to fight it off. My other friend also caught fire while trying to rescue him. It was hard to breathe as I heard their screams of agony. Suddenly, it became completely dark, and my friends' screams stopped. They were gone. The flames grew bigger and the fire surrounded me too. I collapsed to the ground. Some time later, I woke up in [[Level 13]]. After traveling between levels for a few days, I managed to find my way and reached a B.W.O. base. From there, they sent me to the capital. There was no doubt my friends were dead, but I'm not sure if what happened afterward was a hallucination.
Entities
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
Most types of entities can usually be found in the level, the most common of which are [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]. While many types of entities can be found on the level, these entities appear to be completely mutated and some may not even be recognizable. It is presumed that these mutations are caused by the high temperature. Some entities at this level can be seen completely burned. While some of these burned entities can be seen moving, others may be found in different forms, such as being dead. Some wanderers have reported seeing various entities screaming in agony as they burn on the ground. It is known that these entities either die within a few minutes or mutate and start moving.
Colonies and Outposts
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
{{Blurimage|file=1099-5.png|caption=A B.W.O. member trying to put out the fire.}} There are no habitats on the level, but some talented researchers have built research facilities on the level. These are as follows: ===[[B.W.O.]] Base Septem=== To make the level habitable again, B.W.O. established a research and firefighting base. This base has been working for years to put out the fire, but has made no progress. B.W.O. has recently announced that it will stop operations and the base will be closed. ===Researchers of Level 10.99=== The most talented researchers from some groups have gathered together and set up a research facility in the relatively less hot and better-off areas of Level 10.99 and are researching the history, features and quirks of the level. New members are strictly refused.
Theories
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
Separation Theory
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
According to '''Seperation Theory''', '''Level 10.99''' was once a part of [[Level 10]], but was separated from Level 10 by noclipping or some other means, its climate changed rapidly, and it could not withstand these climatic changes so it started to burn. The source of this theory is that [[B.W.O.]] members found a note on the ground supposedly written in [[Level 10]] when the level was first discovered. It is believed that this note was written before Level 10.99 separated from [[Level 10]].
Burning Theory
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
According to the '''Burning Theory''', the level used to be a field similar to [[Level 10]], but someone, or some people, came and burned the level. However, there is no proof to support this theory.
Entrances and Exits
{{Underline|FFFFFF}}
Entrances
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} • Entering a door in [[Level 58]] might bring one here.
• Trying to set a place on fire at [[Level 10]] will bring one here.
• Sleeping at [[Level 10.2]] can bring one here.
Exits
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} • Entering a cave can take one to [[Level 8]].
• Sleeping may take one to [[Level 13]]. * After prolonged exposure to the burning field, wanderers may suddenly find themselves in [[Level 146]]. {{User:Muqon/Credits|image=1099-4.png|author=Muqon|other=
Also, thanks for reading this, {{USERNAME}}! It would be great if you gave me feedback.
